mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 200k persons signals robust labor market expansion and rising consumer purchasing power, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2015 payroll level at 141183k suggests ongoing expansion
failure_condition: Monthly payroll growth exceeds 200k in > 70% of observations yet 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero levels (below 0.25%) constrains further monetary accommodation, signaling an imminent tightening cycle that reduces equity appeal relative to fixed-income alternatives, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 6+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:April 2015 FEDFUNDS at 0.11% near historical floor
regime_hint: late-stage zero lower bound with tightening expectations
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% yet 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, which compresses equity multiples through lower earnings growth expectations over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:April 2015 HOUST at 897k and RSAFS at 441363M
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown with synchronized consumption and investment weakness
failure_condition: Both HOUST and RSAFS decline simultaneously in > 60% of observations yet 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 105 index points indicates robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, supporting corporate profit margins and driving positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO remains above 105 in > 75% of months yet 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 5.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced wage growth expectations, which dampens consumer spending and equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points over 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:April 2015 UNRATE at 5.5% near cyclical lows
failure_condition: UNRATE rises > 0.4pp over 3 months in > 50% of observations yet 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI growth below 2% annually combined with low federal funds rates signals monetary accommodation persistence, reducing real discount rates and supporting equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2015 CPIAUCSL at 235.186 and FEDFUNDS at 0.11%
failure_condition: Both conditions met in > 60% of observations yet 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 below 2.5% annualized signals sub-trend economic expansion and reduced corporate earnings growth, producing negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Annualized GDPC1 growth rate < 2.5% for 2 consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:Q4 2014 GDPC1 at 16294.7B suggests moderate growth
regime_hint: sub-trend growth regime with earnings compression risk
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth < 2.5% annualized in > 50% of 2-quarter windows yet 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 900k units combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) signals strong consumer demand and construction activity, which drives equity valuations through accelerating earnings growth over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2015 HOUST at 897k near 900k threshold and RSAFS at 441363M
regime_hint: demand-side expansion with synchronized consumption and investment strength
failure_condition: Both conditions met in > 65% of observations yet 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
